mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth and increasing retail sales indicate expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 and RSAFS both show sequential growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that typically precedes wage growth and consumer spending acceleration, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not statistically higher than baseline when UNRATE crosses below 6.0% (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and stable consumer prices indicates favorable supply-side conditions with controlled inflation, compressing risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and non-rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 5d returns in >40% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, further increases in the federal funds rate signal aggressive monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive or neutral in >50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL is above 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving business and consumer confidence, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as construction activity and related sector earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where HOUST shows sequential month-over-month increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals an aggressive tightening cycle that typically overshoots economic targets, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >45% of quarters where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25pp within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through stronger consumer spending and corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to yield positive 20d returns in >50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 100.0 during a post-peak contraction phase, it indicates weakening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where INDPRO remains below 100.0 during a confirmed economic contraction.
